圆.origin: An enclosure 囗 wrapped neatly all the way around — round like a coin with no corners.

sentence.en: The moon is big and round today.

冷.origin: Two little ice drops 冫 on the side mean freezing — so 冷 feels nice and cold.

sentence.en: The winter wind is very cold.

跑.origin: A foot 足 on the left gets your legs moving fast — that's how you run!

sentence.en: The puppy runs on the grass.

海.origin: Three water drops 氵 beside 每 — so much water all together makes the great sea.

sentence.en: We go to the seaside to play.

跳.origin: A foot 足 next to 兆 — push off with your feet and jump up high!

sentence.en: The little rabbit hops around.

白.origin: Like a bright ray of sunlight or a glowing candle — clean and shining white.

sentence.en: The clouds in the sky are white.

数.origin: Counting things one by one — 数 means numbers and the maths you do at school.

sentence.en: I like math class.

黑.origin: A face smudged with soot from a fire 灬 below — all dark and black.

sentence.en: At night the sky is very dark.

结.origin: A silk thread 纟 beside 吉 — tie the thread tightly and you make a knot.

sentence.en: He helped me tie my shoelaces.

气.origin: Wavy lines like a breath puffing out — 气 is the air we breathe and the weather.

sentence.en: The weather is very nice today.

被.origin: A cloth 衤 by 皮 — first a warm blanket, now it also means something is done to you.

sentence.en: The flower was blown over by the wind.

首.origin: A head with little horns 丷 on top of a face 自 — 首 means head, or first.

sentence.en: I can sing this song.

王.origin: Three lines joined by one stroke — a person linking heaven and earth: the king.

sentence.en: There is a king in the story.

纸.origin: Silk threads 纟 pressed flat beside 氏 — long ago that's how people made paper.

sentence.en: I draw pictures on the paper.

便.origin: A person 亻 making things easy and handy — 便 means convenient, and also cheap.

sentence.en: This book is very cheap.

决.origin: Two ice drops 冫 beside 夬 — make up your mind firmly, clear as ice: decide!

sentence.en: I decided to go to the park.

明.origin: The sun 日 and the moon 月 standing together — both lights make everything bright.

sentence.en: Tomorrow we go to school.

节.origin: Grass 艹 on top, like a section of bamboo — 节 means a joint, or a festival day.

sentence.en: Today is a happy festival.

闻.origin: An ear 耳 inside a door 门 — listen and you hear the news through the doorway.

sentence.en: I smell the flowers.

城.origin: Earth 土 piled up beside 成 — long ago walls of earth grew into a whole city.

sentence.en: I live in a big city.

合.origin: A lid coming down to cover a mouth 口 — pieces fit and join neatly together.

sentence.en: Everyone works together.

蓝.origin: Grass 艹 on top of 监 — long ago a special plant gave the lovely color blue.

sentence.en: The sky is blue.

既.origin: A person turning away from a full bowl of food — already finished: 既 means since.

sentence.en: He is both tall and fast.

外.origin: Evening 夕 and a divining stick 卜 — fortune-telling at night points to the outside.

sentence.en: It is raining outside.

园.origin: An enclosure 囗 around 元 — a fence circling green space makes a lovely garden.

sentence.en: We play in the park.

万.origin: A single sweeping stroke that stands for a huge number — ten thousand!

sentence.en: There are ten thousand stars in the sky.

脚.origin: The flesh radical ⺼ beside 却 — a part of your body at the bottom: your foot.

sentence.en: My foot hurts a little.

矮.origin: An arrow 矢 beside 委 — measure with a tiny arrow and you find something short.

sentence.en: My little brother is shorter than me.

比.origin: Two little spoons 匕 standing side by side — line them up to compare them.

sentence.en: Let's compare.

喝.origin: A mouth 口 beside 曷 — open wide and sip something tasty: that's to drink.

sentence.en: I drink milk every day.

晚.origin: The sun 日 beside 免 — when the sun slips away, it becomes evening and gets late.

sentence.en: At night I go to sleep early.

聪.origin: An ear 耳 beside 总 — a child who listens well learns fast and is very clever.

sentence.en: This child is really clever.

块.origin: Earth 土 beside 夬 — a lump of ground broken off is a piece, or one dollar.

sentence.en: Give me a piece of cookie.

远.origin: The walking road 辶 beside 元 — keep walking on and on until it gets very far.

sentence.en: The school is far from home.

努.origin: Strength 力 at the bottom — squeeze out all your power and try your very hardest.

sentence.en: We should study hard.

球.origin: The jade radical 王 beside 求 — once a round jade gem, now any round ball.

sentence.en: He is playing with a ball.

漂.origin: Water 氵 beside 票 — things float on the water, and pretty things float into view.

sentence.en: Her dress is very pretty.

环.origin: The jade radical 王 beside 不 — a round jade loop you can wear: a ring or circle.

sentence.en: We should protect the environment.

境.origin: Earth 土 beside 竟 — the land that surrounds you is your place, or a border.

sentence.en: The environment here is beautiful.

船.origin: A little boat 舟 on the left carries you across the water: a ship sails away.

sentence.en: The big ship sails on the sea.
